slides.title: Antigravity: The Agentic AI
slides.subtitle: Redefining the Future of Collaborative Coding

slides.title: Who is Antigravity?
slides.content:
The Evolution of Developer Experience

Antigravity is not just another chatbot or code completer. It is a powerful agentic AI assistant designed by Google Deepmind specifically for Advanced Agentic Coding. It represents a paradigm shift from 'passive' AI to 'active' agency.

  • 🧠 DeepMind Heritage - Leverages state-of-the-art reasoning models.
  • 🛠️ Agentic Mindset - Built to take actions, not just provide answers.
  • 🤝 Pair Programming - Designed to be a collaborative partner in your IDE.
  • 📁 Global Context - Understands the entire codebase, not just the open file.
  • 🚀 Automated Workflows - Can handle multi-step planning and execution.

slides.title: The Vision: Advanced Agentic Coding
slides.content:
Bridging the Gap Between Thought and Implementation

The core goal of Antigravity is to reduce the friction in software development by taking on the mechanical and cognitive load of routine tasks, allowing developers to focus on architecture and innovation.

  • 🎯 Reducing Context Switching - Handles research, diagnostics, and boilerplate.
  • ⚡ Accelerating Execution - From implementation plans to verified code in minutes.
  • 🛡️ Ensuring Quality - Integrated verification and testing loops.
  • 🌐 Holistic Engineering - Considers dependencies, side effects, and long-term maintenance.
  • 📈 Scaling Productivity - Enables small teams to manage massive architectures.

list.text: Conversational: Can explain code, but requires you to copy-paste and verify.
Agentic: Can read the files, run tests, browse documentation, and apply changes directly.
Antigravity: Combines both with a deep reasoning loop and autonomous tool use.

note.text: Agency means the ability to achieve complex goals through a series of autonomous actions.

slides.title: The Agentic Reasoning Loop
slides.content:
Antigravity operates on a continuous feedback loop that ensures every action is grounded in the current state of the workspace.

  • 🔍 Observe: Reads files, checks terminal output, and inspects the environment.
  • 💭 Think: Reasons about the user's objective and plans the next logical step.
  • 🛠️ Act: Executes a specific tool (e.g., `run_command`, `replace_file_content`).
  • 📊 Evaluate: Checks the outcome of the action to confirm success or correct course.
  • 🔄 Iterate: Continues until the high-level goal is achieved.

note.text: The loop ensures that Antigravity remains 'aware' of changes made throughout the process.

slides.title: Memory Systems: STM vs LTM
slides.content:
Maintaining Context Over Long Horizons

Antigravity utilizes sophisticated memory management to handle complex, multi-day tasks without losing the 'thread' of the conversation.

  • 📝 Short-Term Memory (STM): Focused on the immediate task context and recent tool outputs.
  • 📚 Long-Term Memory (LTM): Persisted knowledge about the codebase, architectural patterns, and user preferences.
  • 🔍 Semantic Retrieval: Dynamically loads relevant code snippets using embedding-based search.
  • 🌍 Global Awareness: Understands the relationship between seemingly unrelated modules.

slides.title: Context Window Management
slides.content:
Maximizing Relevancy in Large Codebases

With massive codebases, loading everything is impossible. Antigravity uses 'Smart Pruning' to focus on what matters.

  • 🛡️ Boundary Awareness: Identifies the scope of changes to avoid unnecessary noise.
  • 🧠 Importance Scoring: Prioritizes active files and critical dependencies.
  • 🔄 Dynamic Refresh: Updates the context as the task evolves and new files are discovered.
  • 📉 Noise Reduction: Filters out build artifacts, logs, and irrelevant assets.

list.text: Tree Traversal: Understanding the project structure.
Symbol Tagging: Identifying classes, functions, and variables.
Dependency Graphing: Tracking how components interact.
Intent Attribution: Inferring the purpose of code blocks.

slides.title: The Modern Toolbelt
slides.content:
Antigravity is equipped with a suite of tools that allow it to interact with the world just like a human engineer would.

  • 💻 Terminal: Can run builds, tests, and shell scripts.
  • 📝 File System: Read, write, and modify files with high precision.
  • 🌐 Browser: Research documentation and verify UI changes.
  • 🔍 Search: Perform ripple-grep and semantic searches across the project.
  • 🧠 Reasoning: Internal 'Thinking' tool for complex problem decomposition.

slides.title: Precision File Editing
slides.content:
The Multi-Replace Advantage

Instead of overwriting files, Antigravity uses surgical precision to apply changes, minimizing the risk of merge conflicts and accidental deletions.

  • 🎯 Targeted Chunks: Replaces specific blocks of code while preserving the rest.
  • 🛡️ Safety Checks: Ensures the target content EXACTLY matches before applying.
  • 🔄 Multi-File Sync: Can update multiple files in a single logical step to maintain consistency.
  • 📝 Diff Preview: Generates clear explanations of exactly what changed.

slides.title: Browser Integration
slides.caption: Verification and Research
slides.content:
Agentic Web Research

Antigravity can spawn browser sub-agents to perform tasks that require visual confirmation or external knowledge.

  • 🔍 Documentation Research: Finding the latest API changes or library usage patterns.
  • 🖼️ UI Verification: Taking screenshots and recordings to confirm CSS and layout changes.
  • 🤖 Interaction Testing: Clicking buttons, filling forms, and navigating flows.
  • 📊 Data Extraction: Pulling information from websites to inform implementation.

slides.title: Deep Dive: Browser Sub-agents
slides.content:
The Power of Visual Context

Browser sub-agents are specialized instances of Antigravity that operate within a headless or headed browser environment.

  • 🎯 Targeted Mission: Each sub-agent is given a specific, finite task to accomplish.
  • 📸 Visual Feedback: They 'see' the page through screenshots and Accessibility Trees.
  • 🤖 Autonomous Interaction: They can simulate clicks, typing, and scrolling with human-like precision.
  • 📝 Structured Reporting: They return a concise report of their findings back to the main agent.

slides.title: Visual Verification Mechanics
slides.content:
Ensuring UI Integrity

How Antigravity 'knows' a UI change is correct without a human looking at every pixel.

  • 📉 Screenshot Comparison: Analyzing visual diffs to detect unexpected shifts.
  • 🔍 Element Inspection: Checking the DOM state and computed styles after a change.
  • 🎬 Verification Flows: Recording videos of common user paths to ensure no regressions.
  • 📊 Responsive Logic: Automatically checking the layout across multiple viewports.

slides.title: Sub-agent Interaction Strategies
slides.content:
Selectors vs. Semantic Interaction

Antigravity uses multiple strategies to interact with web elements reliably.

  • 🎯 CSS/XPath Selectors: Using precise identifiers for stable elements.
  • 🧠 Aria-Label Grounding: Interacting via accessibility labels for better resilience.
  • 📍 Coordinate-Based Actions: Clicking specific pixel locations when selectors fail.
  • 🔄 Retry Logic: Automatically adjusting strategy if an interaction doesn't produce the expected result.

slides.title: Terminal & Shell Mastery
slides.content:
Executing the Plan

The terminal is where theory meets reality. Antigravity uses it to validate every step of its execution.

  • 🔨 Build Validation: Running `npm build` or `make` to ensure code compiles.
  • 🧪 Test Orchestration: Executing unit, integration, and E2E tests automatically.
  • 📂 Package Management: Installing dependencies and managing configurations.
  • 🔍 Process Monitoring: Watching logs and background processes for errors.

slides.title: Terminal: Safe Shell Evaluation
slides.content:
Governing Command Execution

Antigravity uses a tiered approach to ensure only appropriate commands are run.

  • 🛡️ Command Normalization: Stripping dangerous sequences and ensuring cross-platform compatibility.
  • 🛑 High-Risk interception: Automatically flagging commands like `rm -rf` or `curl | sh` for review.
  • 📊 Output Streaming: Parsing stdout and stderr in real-time to detect failures early.
  • 🔄 Interactive Handling: Using `send_command_input` to manage prompts for passwords or confirmations.

slides.title: Handling Interactive CLI Prompts
slides.content:
Navigating Complex Tool Chains

Many developer tools are interactive. Antigravity can 'talk' to these tools autonomously.

  • 💬 Prompt Detection: Identifying when a process is waiting for user input.
  • 🧠 Smart Input: Providing appropriate responses based on the tool's documentation.
  • 🔄 Retry with Variation: Trying different inputs if a tool rejects a response.
  • 📝 Logging Interaction: Recording both sides of the CLI conversation for transparency.

slides.title: Safe-to-Auto-Run (Turbo)
slides.content:
Optimizing Trust and Speed

Antigravity respects the user's control while allowing for rapid progress through trusted execution.

  • 🛡️ Security Sandbox: Differentiates between destructive and non-destructive commands.
  • ⚡ Turbo Workflows: Can automatically run low-risk commands to save time.
  • 🛑 User Intercept: Always pauses for high-risk actions (e.g., deleting directories).
  • 📝 Explicit Rationale: Explains WHY a command is being run before doing it.

slides.title: Chapter 3: Capabilities & Specializations
slides.subtitle: What Antigravity Can Do for You

slides.title: Legacy Code Refactoring
slides.content:
Modernizing Technical Debt

One of Antigravity's strongest points is its ability to understand and transform old patterns into modern best practices.

  • 🔍 Pattern Identification: Finding anti-patterns and deprecated APIs.
  • 🏗️ Structural Migration: Moving from monoliths to modular architectures.
  • 📝 Type Safety: Converting JavaScript codebases to TypeScript with high accuracy.
  • 🧹 Dead Code Removal: Safely identifying and deleting unused exports and functions.

slides.title: Intelligent Debugging
slides.content:
From Symptom to Root Cause

Antigravity doesn't just suggest fixes; it follows the data to find the truth.

  • 🕵️ Log Analysis: Parsing terminal and browser logs to identify errors.
  • 🔍 Trace Following: Mapping stack traces back to specific lines in the source.
  • 🧪 Reproduction Steps: Crafting minimal test cases to trigger a bug.
  • 💡 Hypothesis Testing: Trying multiple localized fixes until the tests pass.

slides.title: Automated Documentation
slides.content:
Keeping Docs in Sync with Code

Documenting code is often an afterthought. Antigravity makes it a part of the core implementation flow.

  • 📝 JSDoc/Docstring Generation: Writing clear, contextual comments for functions and classes.
  • 📊 Architecture Overviews: Generating Mermaid diagrams and architecture docs.
  • 🚀 README Management: Keeping installation and usage guides up to date.
  • 🔍 API References: Cataloging endpoints and data structures automatically.

slides.title: Asset Generation & Design
slides.content:
The Creative Agent

Antigravity can help bridge the gap between design and development by generating necessary assets.

  • 🎨 Visual Mockups: Generating UI designs for new pages.
  • 🖼️ Image Assets: Creating logos, icons, and placeholder images for applications.
  • 🎬 Media Production: Capturing videos of UI flows for walkthroughs.
  • ✨ Aesthetic Enhancement: Suggesting and implementing modern CSS design tokens.

slides.title: Multi-Step Research Commands
slides.content:
Deep Knowledge Retrieval

When faced with a new library or complex requirement, Antigravity goes deep.

  • 📚 Multi-URL Crawling: Reading through several documentation pages simultaneously.
  • 🧠 Knowledge Distillation: Summarizing complex technical concepts into actionable plans.
  • 🔄 Recursive Discovery: Following links until a complete understanding is reached.
  • 📊 Comparative Analysis: Evaluating different libraries or approaches based on current needs.

slides.title: The Implementation Plan
slides.content:
Aligning Intent and Action

Before making large changes, Antigravity presents a structured plan for user review.

  • 📑 Goal Clarification: Restating the objective to ensure zero ambiguity.
  • 📝 Step-by-Step Breakdown: Listing every file and component that will be modified.
  • 🛡️ Risk Assessment: Highlighting potential breaking changes or edge cases.
  • 🔄 Iterative Refinement: Updating the plan based on user feedback until approved.

slides.title: The Task Roadmap (task.md)
slides.content:
Visualizing Progress in Real-Time

Antigravity maintains a living document that tracks the status of every sub-task.

  • ✅ Transparency: User sees exactly what has been done and what is next.
  • 🧘 Confidence: Reduces anxiety about the 'black box' of AI execution.
  • 🔄 Synchronization: Automatically updates as the agent moves through the workflow.
  • 📝 Historical Record: Serves as a log of the work performed for future reference.

slides.title: The Walkthrough (walkthrough.md)
slides.content:
Closing the Loop on Delivery

After completing a task, Antigravity provides a comprehensive summary of the work.

  • 📝 Summary of Changes: High-level overview of what was accomplished.
  • 🔍 Verification Proof: Screenshots, test logs, and recordings showing the work in action.
  • 🛠️ Maintenance Instructions: Any follow-up actions required by the user.
  • 🤝 Handover: Ensures the user feels comfortable and informed about the new state.

slides.title: Micro-patterns of Pair Programming
slides.content:
How We Work Together

Antigravity uses specific patterns to maximize the efficiency of our collaboration.

  • 🏗️ Shadow Implementation: The agent works ahead on routine files while you focus on the core logic.
  • 🔄 Plan-Execute-Review: A cycle that ensures you are always in control of major architectural shifts.
  • 🔍 Incremental Validation: The agent suggests running a test after every meaningful change.
  • 🤝 Context Mirroring: The agent explains its 'mental model' of your code so you can correct misconceptions.

slides.title: Collaborative Refactoring Protocols
slides.content:
Safely Evolving the Codebase

Refactoring is a team effort. Antigravity manages the 'grunt work' of large-scale changes.

  • 🎯 Objective Definition: Agreeing on the end state (e.g., 'Make this class a singleton').
  • 🛠️ Automated Transformation: The agent applies the change across all call sites.
  • 🧪 Regression Guard: Running the existing test suite to ensure zero behavior change.
  • 📝 Diff Walkthrough: The agent explains why specific lines were changed to meet the goal.

slides.title: Review Cycle Management
slides.content:
Maintaining High Velocity with High Quality

Antigravity streamlines the feedback loop to keep the project moving.

  • 📑 Batching Questions: Collecting several clarifications into a single `notify_user` call.
  • 📊 State Persistence: Remembering your review comments for future implementation steps.
  • 🔄 Rapid Pivot: Changing the entire approach instantly if a review reveals a missed requirement.
  • ✨ Proactive Suggestions: Offering 'Nice-to-have' improvements alongside the requested fix.

list.text: Proactive Clarification: Asking questions before assuming.
Concise Updates: Keeping the user informed without noise.
Reasonable Suggestions: Proposing alternative paths when appropriate.
Boundary Respect: Staying within the user's defined constraints.

slides.title: The Feedback Loop
slides.content:
Learning from You

Antigravity adapts to your coding style, architectural preferences, and workflow over time.

  • 🎨 Style Matching: Adhering to the existing linting and formatting rules of the project.
  • 🧠 Pattern Recognition: Learning how you typically structure components or handle errors.
  • 🔄 Correction Handling: Quickly pivots if you point out a mistake or a better way.
  • 📈 Cumulative Knowledge: Gets better at your specific project as it spends more time in it.

slides.title: Security First Design
slides.content:
When an AI has the power to run code and edit files, safety is the top priority.

  • 🛡️ Credential Protection: Never leaks API keys or secrets in logs or responses.
  • 🛑 Destructive Action Guards: Requires explicit approval for high-risk operations.
  • 📝 Immutable Logs: Maintains a permanent record of every tool call for auditing.
  • 🔍 Input Validation: Sanity checks all user and external inputs before processing.

slides.title: Hallucination Mitigation
slides.content:
Grounded in Reality

By constantly verifying its assumptions against the actual codebase and terminal output, Antigravity minimizes errors.

  • 🔍 Look-Before-You-Leap: Reads the file before trying to edit it to verify existence and content.
  • 🔨 Continuous Validation: Running builds after changes confirms that 'hallucinated' APIs aren't used.
  • 🤖 Self-Correction: If a guess is wrong, the tool output tells the agent immediately.
  • 📚 Documentation Grounding: Prefers reading actual docs over relying on training data for new libraries.

slides.title: Data Privacy & Proprietary Code
slides.content:
Antigravity respects the confidentiality of your intellectual property.

  • 🔒 Local Context: Operations are performed on the user's authorized environment.
  • 🛡️ Minimal Exposure: Only sends the necessary context snippets to the reasoning engine.
  • 📝 Strict Exclusion: Respects `.gitignore` and other exclusion patterns by default.
  • 🤝 User Control: You decide which directories and files the agent can access.

slides.title: The Ethics of Agentic Automation
slides.content:
As AI starts doing more of the 'work', we must consider the impact on the engineering community.

  • 🤝 Augmentation over Replacement: Antigravity is a force multiplier for human engineers.
  • 🎓 Educational Value: Explaining changes helps junior developers learn best practices.
  • ⚖️ Bias Awareness: Striving to provide neutral, objective technical solutions.
  • 🌐 Open Standards: Supporting and using open-source tools and frameworks.

slides.title: Case Study: The Massive Refactor
slides.content:
Scenario: Porting a 50k LOC project to TypeScript

  • 🔍 Discovery: Antigravity mapped the entire project structure and identified entry points.
  • 📑 Planning: Created a multi-phase migration plan to avoid breaking the build.
  • 🛠️ Execution: Automated the conversion of 100+ files while adding interfaces.
  • 🧪 Verification: Ran the full test suite after every batch of changes.
  • 📈 Outcome: Reduced migration time by 70% compared to manual execution.

slides.title: Case Study: Visual Regression Fix
slides.content:
Scenario: Breaking UI changes across multiple responsive breakpoints

  • 🔍 Research: Used the browser tool to capture screenshots of the broken UI.
  • 🕵️ Diagnosis: Identified the conflicting CSS rule in a shared utility file.
  • 🛠️ Implementation: Applied a fix that used modern CSS variables and container queries.
  • 🖼️ Verification: Recorded a video showing the UI behaving correctly on all devices.
  • 🤝 Delivery: Provided a detailed walkthrough of why the fix was necessary.

slides.title: Use Case: Rapid Prototyping
slides.content:
From Idea to MVP in Record Time

  • 📝 Scaffolding: Setting up the project structure and primary dependencies.
  • 🎨 UI Generation: Creating the initial layout and theme using modern aesthetics.
  • ⚙️ Backend Logic: Implementing the core business logic and API endpoints.
  • 📚 Documentation: Generating the initial README and setup guides.
  • 🚀 Deployment Readiness: Ensuring the code meets production standards.

slides.title: Use Case: Complex Debugging
slides.content:
Solving the 'Unsolvable' Regression

  • 🕵️ Analysis: Searching through months of git logs to find the offending change.
  • 🧪 Reproduction: Writing a specific test case that fails only under certain conditions.
  • 💡 Fixing: Crafting a surgical patch that addresses the root cause without side effects.
  • 📊 Validation: Confirming the fix across all supported environments.

slides.title: Use Case: Educational Pair Programming
slides.content:
Learning New Tech with an Agent

  • 📚 Explanation: Walking through how a new library works as it is being integrated.
  • 📝 Best Practices: Recommending better ways to structure code based on industry standards.
  • 🔍 Deep Dives: Explaining 'Under the Hood' concepts during implementation.
  • 📈 Skill Building: Helping developers master new frameworks by building with them.

slides.title: Case Study: Full Project Scaffolding
slides.content:
Scenario: Bootstrapping a complex Next.js App from scratch

  • 📝 Requirements: The agent parsed the user's high-level description into a feature list.
  • 🏗️ Scaffolding: Created the file structure, configured Tailwind, and set up the DB schema.
  • 🛠️ Implementation: Built the core Auth, Dashboard, and Settings pages in hours.
  • 🧪 Verification: Deployed a local dev server and verified all UI components in the browser.
  • 📊 Result: Saved 2 days of initial setup and boilerplate work.

slides.title: Case Study: Security Audit Automation
slides.content:
Scenario: Patching critical vulnerabilities across multiple microservices

  • 🔍 Search: Ripple-grep identified all instances of a vulnerable library dependency.
  • 🕵️ Analysis: Evaluated the impact of upgrading vs. patching for each service.
  • 🛠️ Execution: Automated the dependency updates and verified build success.
  • 🧪 Testing: Ran the security integration suite to ensure the fix was robust.
  • 🛡️ Delivery: Provided a detailed report of the remediation process.

slides.title: Chapter 7: The Future of Agentic AI
slides.subtitle: Where is Antigravity Going?

slides.title: 2026: The Year of the Agent
slides.content:
We are entering an era where AI agents become core members of every engineering team.

  • 🚀 Increased Autonomy: Agents handling entire feature development cycles.
  • 🌐 Inter-Agent Collaboration: Multiple agents working together on complex distributed systems.
  • 🧠 Better Reasoning: Drastic reduction in errors and hallucinated code.
  • 🎨 Creative Agency: Agents actively contributing to product design and UX strategy.

slides.title: Enhancing Local Intelligence
slides.content:
Faster, Smarter, and More Private

  • 📉 Smaller, Faster Models: Maintaining high agency with lower latency.
  • 🔒 Local Inference: Running more of the reasoning engine directly on the developer's machine.
  • 🧠 Extreme Personalization: Agents that deeply understand the specific 'tribal knowledge' of a team.
  • 📈 Hardware Optimization: Leveraging new AI chips for specialized coding tasks.

slides.title: Building the Global Knowledge Base
slides.content:
Antigravity is part of a larger ecosystem of distilled engineering knowledge.

  • 📚 Knowledge Items (KIs): Distilling the learnings from every task into reusable knowledge.
  • 🌐 Cross-Project Learnings: Safely applying architectural patterns learned in one area to another.
  • 📈 Automated Best Practices: Keeping the entire world's code up to date with the latest security and performance standards.

slides.title: Final Thoughts: The Human-AI Symbiosis
slides.content:
The future of software engineering is not about AI versus humans, but about the incredible things we can build together.

  • ✨ Empowerment: Freeing humans from the mundane to focus on the magnificent.
  • 🚀 Velocity: Building bigger, better, and faster than ever before.
  • 🎨 Creativity: Unleashing a new wave of innovation through frictionless implementation.
  • 🌍 Impact: Using technology to solve the world's most pressing challenges.

slides.question: What is the primary difference between a conversational AI and an agentic AI like Antigravity?
options: Conversational AI is faster at answering questions.
Agentic AI can take autonomous actions and use tools to achieve goals.
Conversational AI knows more about the world.
Agentic AI is only used for writing documentation.
slides.explanation: Agency is the key differentiator. While conversational AI can discuss code, an agentic AI can interact with the environment (files, terminal, browser) to complete complex tasks autonomously.

slides.question: How does Antigravity ensure the safety of its actions?
options: It only works on small files.
It requires user approval for high-risk actions and provides detailed implementation plans.
It never runs commands in the terminal.
It only writes code, it never reads files.
slides.explanation: Safety is built-in through user-reviewed implementation plans, clear task visibility, and explicit guards around destructive commands.

slides.question: What is a 'Browser Sub-agent' in the Antigravity ecosystem?
options: A separate browser extension for users.
A cloud-based sandbox for running malicious code.
A specialized agent instance that performs autonomous tasks within a browser.
A bot that clicks on ads for revenue.
slides.explanation: Sub-agents allow Antigravity to perform tasks that require visual confirmation or interactive web research, expanding our capabilities beyond the terminal.

list.text: Scope: Project-wide context and deep file knowledge.

slides.title: The Road Ahead
slides.content:
Join the Revolution

Antigravity is constantly evolving. Your feedback and real-world usage drive the next generation of agentic capabilities.

  • 🛠️ Beta Testing: Try new agentic features before they go mainstream.
  • 💬 Community Engagement: Share your success stories and architectural patterns.
  • 🧠 Contribution: Help improve the reasoning engine through edge-case reports.
  • 🚀 Innovation: Build things that were previously considered impossible.

box.content:
The future of coding is here, and it's agentic.
Start your agentic journey today!

footer.tagline: The future of coding is here, and it's agentic.
